---
title: ""
page-layout: custom
---

::: {.grid .profile-grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4 .profile-sidebar}

### Audrey Etienne

**Assistant Professor in Economics**\
[LERN](https://lern.univ-rouen.fr/) — Université Rouen Normandie

✉ [audrey.etienne3@univ-rouen.fr](mailto:audrey.etienne3@univ-rouen.fr)\
🔗 [GitHub](https://github.com/audreyetienne3)\
📚 [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=-sLoKqMAAAAJ&hl=en)\
📄 [HAL](https://cv.hal.science/audrey-etienne)\
🆔 [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0003-4386-6031)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8 .profile-main}

## About

I am an assistant professor (*Maître de conférences*) in economics at the Université Rouen Normandie, affiliated with [LERN](https://lern.univ-rouen.fr/).

My research sits at the intersection of labor economics, applied microeconomics, and public economics, with a focus on discrimination, gender inequality, and meritocracy.

I hold a PhD in Economics from [Aix-Marseille School of Economics](https://www.amse-aixmarseille.fr/) (2018, dir. O. Bargain), a Master from [Paris School of Economics](https://www.parisschoolofeconomics.eu/) (PPD), and a Master in Management from [ESCP Europe](https://escp.eu/) (Programme Grande École). Before joining Rouen, I was a postdoctoral researcher at OFCE–Sciences Po and at DREES (French Ministry of Health).

## Research interests

- Discrimination & gender inequality
- Moral hazard & information economics
- Household finance & within-couple inequality
- Political economy of populism
- Public-private sector wage gaps

## News

- **2026** — "Is the Public Sector Losing the Battle for Talent?" published in *The Economic Journal* (with O. Bargain & B. Melly)
- **2025** — "Blind Auditions?" published in *Travail, genre et sociétés* (with H. Ravet, R. Hatzipetrou-Andronikou, M. Parodi & H. Périvier)
<!-- TODO: Ajoute d'autres actualités -->

:::

:::
